steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $10x + 30$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Apply the distributive property
steps.content: Multiply 10 by each term inside the parentheses: $$10(x + 3) = 10 \cdot x + 10 \cdot 3 = 10x + 30$$

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
• A: $10x + 3$ — Only the $x$ was multiplied by 10; the constant 3 was left unchanged. Eliminated.
  • B: $10x + 13$ — The 3 was added to 10 rather than multiplied. Eliminated.
  • C: $10x + 7$ — No arithmetic operation on $x + 3$ produces a constant of 7. Eliminated.
  • D: $10x + 30$ — Both terms correctly multiplied by 10. That is the correct answer.
